{{Infobox location
| name = Free Worlds
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Free Worlds Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Free [[Worlds]] consist of a cluster of planets that have maintained their independence by remaining outside the reach of the [[Galactic Union]]. While they lack a central inter-system government, the various planetary governments within this territory value their autonomy and coordinate to defend one another during attacks. This collective force is represented by organized battlegroups of ships. Unlike the uniform, metal-ringed colonies of the [[Union]], the Free [[Worlds]] feature more diverse and individualistic structures, such as mining colonies built into mountains. Historically, the region struggled with security when single-planet militaries proved insufficient to maintain order; this led to the rise of the Advocates. Originally a group of vigilantes, the Advocates eventually assumed quasi-official authority to provide protection and services for those outside conventional law enforcement. However, the stability of the Free [[Worlds]] has been impacted by the fall of the Advocates and subsequent corporate scrambling following their decline.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Denon Javal takes odd jobs across the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] appears to be abandoning the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c37">''The Captain'', chapter 37</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Situated on the fringes of civilized space as one of the four corners of the galaxy, the Free [[Worlds]] is a political entity consisting of planets such as [[Murrok]] and various uncharted worlds. This region, which includes [[Carturus]] and borders the [[Scylon system]], lacks a higher inter-system government but is significantly controlled by the Advocates through their representative battlegroups. While the [[High Council]] receives video reports from this area, the Free [[Worlds]] is characterized by a hatred for the [[Vallenar Corporation]] and core sectors that are swarmed by red dots on sector maps. As planets closest to the region are targeted, the crew finds themselves making their way toward the territory, even as the narrator intends to avoid the area until stability returns. Ultimately, the crew must leave the region, which remains a place where they might encounter [[Starhammer]] again.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] are located on the fringes of civilized space.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c1">''The Engineer'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Advocates]] have significant control over the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6">''The Engineer'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] are a region from which video reports are received by the [[High Council]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Carturus]] is within the territory of the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Scylon system]] is on the border of the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10">''The Engineer'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] is a location the narrator intends to avoid until things calm down.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] is a location where the characters might encounter [[Starhammer]] again.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c23">''The Engineer'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] is a region or political entity.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29">''The Engineer'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] consist of planets like [[Murrok]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The crew needs to leave the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] have no higher inter-system government.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31">''The Engineer'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] hate the [[Vallenar Corporation]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] are represented by battlegroups in the [[Advocates' force]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] is one of the four corners of the galaxy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34">''The Engineer'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] is a group that populations in the [[Thevellian System]] would likely join.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c35">''The Engineer'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Free Worlds]] contains uncharted planets.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c40">''The Engineer'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The core sectors of the [[Free Worlds]] are swarmed by red dots on a sector map.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41">''The Engineer'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The planets closest to the [[Free Worlds]] are being targeted first.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45">''The Engineer'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The crew members were making their way toward the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50">''The Engineer'', chapter 50</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Free Worlds]] hate the crew of [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c19">''The Knight'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Free Worlds]] contain a minor mechanical supply company.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c45">''The Knight'', chapter 45</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Free Worlds]] are a region or group of worlds mentioned in relation to the [[Zenith]] Starship's destruction.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c6">''The Pilot'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Last Horizon]] attacked the [[Free Worlds]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“Just on the border of the Free Worlds.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“You know as well as I do that if we stay in the Free Worlds, we’re going to end up against Starhammer again.”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c23" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“As expected,” Horizon said, “they have begun with the planets closest to the Free Worlds.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon locations]]
